perturbation.original_text: 8. MAXIMUM DAILY QUANTITY (Dth/Day): 101,300 Please see Appendix A for further detail.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. MAXIMUM DAILY QUANTITY (Dth/Day): Please see Appendix A for further detail.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of '101,300' Dth/Day creates uncertainty regarding the Maximum Daily Quantity. While the section refers to Appendix A for details, without the original numerical value, the agreement lacks clarity on the actual quantity commitment. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because other sections might rely on this numerical value, and without it, their interpretation becomes ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX A Contract Identification FT18150 Date: November 01, 2016 Supersedes Appendix Dated: November 01, 2015 Shipper: ANR PIPELINE COMPANY Maximum Daily Quantity (Dth/Day) per Location: Begin Date End Date Point(s) of Primary Receipt Point(s) of Primary Delivery MDQ Maximum Allowable Operating Pressure (MAOP) 11/01/2014 03/31/2015 SOUTH CHESTER 101,300 974 11/01/2014 03/31/2015 DEWARD 101,300 974 04/01/2015 10/31/2015 SOUTH CHESTER 0 974 11/01/2015 03/31/2016 SOUTH CHESTER 101,300 974 11/01/2015 03/31/2016 DEWARD 101,300 974 04/01/2016 10/31/2016 SOUTH CHESTER 0 974 11/01/2016 03/31/2017 SOUTH CHESTER 101,300 974 11/01/2016 03/31/2017 DEWARD 101,300 974 04/01/2017 10/31/2017 SOUTH CHESTER 0 974 11/01/2014 03/31/2015 FARWELL 101,300 974 04/01/2015 10/31/2015 FARWELL 0 974 11/01/2015 03/31/2016 FARWELL 101,300 974 04/01/2016 10/31/2016 FARWELL 0 974 11/01/2016 03/31/2017 FARWELL 101,300 974 04/01/2017 10/31/2017 FARWELL 0 974 Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016 QuickLinks Exhibit 99.12 TRANSPORTATION SERVICE AGREEMENT Contract Identification FT18150 APPENDIX A Contract Identification FT18150 Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states '101,300 Dth/Day' as the maximum daily quantity, while the changed text omits this value, referring only to Appendix A. Appendix A lists specific MDQ values for different time periods and locations, including '101,300' and '0'. The omission of the numerical value in the changed text creates a direct contradiction. Without the explicit '101,300 Dth/Day' in the main text, it is unclear if the '0' values in Appendix A are permitted or if the '101,300' value is still relevant for all periods. This creates uncertainty and inconsistency, directly contradicting the information provided in Appendix A, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. RATES: Unless Shipper and Transporter have agreed to a rate other than the maximum rate, rates shall be Transporter's maximum rates and charges plus all applicable surcharges in effect from time to time under the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) on file with the Commission unless otherwise agreed to by the parties in writing. Provisions governing a Rate other than the maximum shall be set forth in this Paragraph 9 and/or on Appendix B hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. RATES: Rates shall be determined as agreed to by the parties in writing. Provisions governing rates shall be set forth in this Paragraph 9 and/or on Appendix B hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that dictates rates shall be the 'Transporter's maximum rates and charges plus all applicable surcharges' unless otherwise agreed, the agreement now solely relies on written agreements between parties. This removal leads to an in-text contradiction because the document lacks an explicit default rate in the absence of a written agreement. This omission introduces ambiguity, as it is unclear how rates will be determined if the parties have not explicitly agreed in writing and potentially contradicting the incorporated FERC Gas Tariff (defined in Section 12).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions" and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time. Transporter may file and seek Commission approval under Section 4 of the Natural Gas Act (NGA) at any time and from time to time to change any rates, charges or provisions set forth in the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) and the "General Terms and Conditions" in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, and Transporter shall have the right to place such changes in effect in accordance with the NGA, and this Agreement shall be deemed to include such changes and any such changes Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016 which become effective by operation of law and Commission Order, without prejudice to Shipper's right to protest the same.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a default rate ('Transporter's maximum rates and charges plus all applicable surcharges') unless otherwise agreed in writing. The changed text removes this default, stating that rates shall be 'determined as agreed to by the parties in writing.' This creates a contradiction with Section 12, which incorporates the Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, including applicable Rate Schedules. If the parties do not agree in writing, there is no rate defined in the changed text, conflicting with the incorporated Tariff which *does* define rates. This omission introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the assumption in Section 12 that the tariff applies.

perturbation.original_text: 15. NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS: All notices and communications with respect to this Agreement shall be in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means as agreed to by the parties, and sent to the addresses stated below or to any other such address(es) as may be designated in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means similarly agreed to: ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS Great Lakes Gas Transmission Limited Partnership Commercial Services 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 ANR PIPELINE COMPANY 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 Attn:
perturbation.changed_text: 15. NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS: All notices and communications with respect to this Agreement shall be sent to the addresses stated below or to any other such address(es) as may be designated: ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS Great Lakes Gas Transmission Limited Partnership Commercial Services 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 ANR PIPELINE COMPANY 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 Attn:
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause 'shall be in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means as agreed to by the parties, and' and 'in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means similarly agreed to', the agreement no longer explicitly dictates the method of communication, nor does it allow for alternative methods agreed upon in writing. The agreement states that notices should be sent to specific addresses, but removes any explicit allowance for communicating through other means. This creates an in-text contradiction by creating uncertainty regarding permissible means of communication and also create doubt to what the addresses stated below are reffering to and how they are supposed to be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS: All notices and communications with respect to this Agreement shall be sent to the addresses stated below or to any other such address(es) as may be designated: ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS Great Lakes Gas Transmission Limited Partnership Commercial Services 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 ANR PIPELINE COMPANY 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 Attn:
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'shall be in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means as agreed to by the parties, and' from section 15 creates a contradiction. The original text specifies acceptable methods for notices and communications. The changed text, replicated as the contradicted text, eliminates these specifications, leading to uncertainty about permissible communication methods and whether the addresses mentioned are the only means of communications. This creates a direct conflict as the changed text fails to define the method of communication, leading to potential disputes.
